64.1,-21.8.p: He remained on board his ship for a time because he could not get a horse to suit him. There was a man named Sveinn who dwelt at Bakki up from Thingnes. He was a good bondi and a merry companion; he often composed verses which it was a delight to listen to.

64.1,-21.8.ch: CHAPTER XLVII. GRETTIR RETURNS TO BJARG--SVEINN AND HIS HORSE
64.1,-21.8.p: Once Grettir left Vellir in the night because he did not wish the traders to know of it. He got a black cape and put it over his clothes to conceal himself. He went up past Thingnes to Bakki, by which time it was light.

64.1,-21.8.ch: CHAPTER XLVIII. DEATH OF THORBJORN OXMAIN
64.1,-21.8.p: Then Grettir rode to the West across the Laxardal Heath and did not stop before he reached Ljarskogar, where he stayed some time in the autumn with Thorsteinn Kuggason. CHAPTER XLIX.

64.1,-21.8.ch: CHAPTER XLIX. GRETTIR VISITS THORSTEINN KUGGASON AND SNORRI GODI
64.1,-21.8.p: This is the proof that he was killed there and not in Midfitjar, as has been elsewhere asserted. Thorbjorn's kinsmen learned of Grettir's being in Ljarskogar and called together their men with the purpose of going there.

64.1,-21.8.ch: CHAPTER XLIX. GRETTIR VISITS THORSTEINN KUGGASON AND SNORRI GODI
64.1,-21.8.p: Grettir then went West to Reykjanes. The men of Hrutafjord came with their followers to Samsstad, where they heard that Grettir had left Ljarskogar, and went back home. CHAPTER L.

64.1,-21.8.ch: CHAPTER LIII. GRETTIR WINTERS IN LJARSKOGAR WITH THORSTEINN KUGGASON
64.1,-21.8.p: GRETTIR WINTERS IN LJARSKOGAR WITH THORSTEINN KUGGASON During the autumn Grettir returned to the South and did not stop till he came to his kinsman Thorsteinn Kuggason in Ljarskogar, who welcomed him.

64.1,-21.8.ch: CHAPTER LIII. GRETTIR WINTERS IN LJARSKOGAR WITH THORSTEINN KUGGASON
64.1,-21.8.p: GRETTIR WINTERS IN LJARSKOGAR WITH THORSTEINN KUGGASON During the autumn Grettir returned to the South and did not stop till he came to his kinsman Thorsteinn Kuggason in Ljarskogar, who welcomed him.

65.9,-19.4.ch: CHAPTER LXI. GRETTIR WINTERS UNDER THE GEITLAND GLACIER
65.9,-19.4.p: Nothing particular occurred that winter, and Grettir found it so dull that he could not stay there any longer. He left the valley and went to the South through the glacier, reaching the middle of Skjaldbreid from the North.
